title: Memory allocations should not be vulnerable to Denial of Service attacks
description: By repeatedly requesting a feature that consumes a lot of memory, attackers can constantly occupy an important part of an application’s hosting server memory. Depending on the application’s deployment architecture, hosting server resources and attackers' capabilities, this can lead to an exhaustion of the available server’s memory. What is the potential impact? A server that faces a memory exhaustion situation can become unstable.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Methods with validation: if (size > MAX_SIZE) return
logic.reasoning: This rule detects potential DoS vulnerabilities where memory allocations use sizes from method parameters without validation. The logic targets MethodDeclaration nodes containing array allocations (new Type[size]) where the size appears to be a variable (not a literal constant). It excludes allocations with constant sizes, named constants (MaxSize, MAX_), and methods that contain validation (if statements in the source). The exclusion patterns prevent false positives in logging, testing, and display contexts.
